mission.title: helm: Implement changes proposed in HIP 6: OCI Support
mission.description:
For more information, please see the following URL: https://github.com/helm/community/blob/main/hips/hip-0006.md

Note: OCI support remains experimental, and you are still required to set HELM_EXPERIMENTAL_OCI=1 in your environment.

**What this PR does / why we need it**:

This PR implements the changes outlined in HIP 6. This will provide a more stable foundation for Helm's OCI registry support.

This adds a new `helm push` subcommand and `Pusher` interface for uploading charts, which can be e

steps.title: Apply the configuration
steps.description:
Apply the following configuration to your cluster:
```yaml
helm install oci://example.com/charts/nginx --version 0.1.0
```

steps.title: Verify the fix
steps.description: Confirm that the issue is resolved in your environment by testing the affected functionality.

resolution.summary:
Running `helm dep update` gives an error but still succeeds
```
helm dep update
Getting updates for unmanaged Helm repositories...
...Unable to get an update from the "oci://localhost:5000/myuser" chart repository:
	object required
Hang tight while we grab the latest from your chart repositories...
Update Complete. ⎈Happy Helming!⎈
Saving 1 charts
Downloading mychart from repo oci://localhost:5000/myuser
Pulled: localhost:5000/myuser/mychart:0.1.0
Digest: sha256:6cb1fa62c9df1f962093a528b34a0f197e68b46a2656764db62b068cd6c56890
Deleting outdated charts
```
codeSnippets:
? Or just let it go?
@jdolitsky I'm not ignoring your question. I was hoping a maintainer would chime in. I did raise this at the last helm meeting and they noted it was worth more thought.
Good point.

It's a bit of an interesting situation... We exceedingly marked it as experimental, though it somehow went through code review and ended up in a public package.

[Our compatibility contract](https://github.com/helm/helm/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#semantic-versioning) states that changes SHOULD remain backward compatible. The keyword SHOULD was carefully considered for cases like this. In this particular case (an experimental flag), I think we can make the exception to remove it entirely. Had the experiment failed and we had to remove the experiment from the Helm project, we would've had to do the same thing anyways. /shrug

I'd be comfortable with removing it entirely this time. But we should be more strict about putting experimental APIs in `internal` moving forward.

I'll let other maintainers chime in here and see what they think - I'm sure community members relying on these APIs may have opinions as well, so I'd ask around.
As far as [HIP 4](https://github.com/helm/community/pull/145) is concerned:

> * Experimental features are not required to ensure backward compatibility for their feature set. (They cannot, however, break backward compatibility for other parts of Helm.) Thus, a release new release of an existing experimental feature may break APIs, change its chart representations, or modify its command-line flags as long as it does not break the compatibility of non-experimental features.

Following that guideline, new releases of experimental features may break backwards compatibility.
Looking at the files added to public package (`/pkg`), they are experimental and can be removed cleanly without affecting the other supported APIs. It is unfortunate that they ended up in the public package but it doesn't break backwards compatibility per se. I would have no objection removing them.
Running `helm dep update` gives an error but still succeeds

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with helm installed or the issue environment reproducible.
